skill.name: llm-evaluation
skill.description: Design evaluation workflows for LLM applications using automated metrics, human review, LLM-as-judge methods, A/B tests, and regression checks.
skill.summary: Build practical evaluation plans for LLM applications and prompt changes.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review shows they are false positives from markdown code fences, metric labels, form scale labels, and dictionary key iteration. The skill is documentation-only, with one low-risk caution: example LLM-as-judge code would send evaluation inputs and outputs to an external API if users copy it into their own application.

low_findings.title: External API Submission in Example Code
low_findings.description: The LLM-as-judge examples call an external OpenAI chat completion API with evaluation prompts that include user questions and model responses. This is a legitimate evaluation pattern, but users should avoid sending confidential test data without approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code examples clearly submit prompt content to an external API, but the skill file is documentation and does not execute this code by itself.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to markdown Python code block delimiters and example Python snippets. No Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a markdown code fence boundary or documentation example, not executable Ruby or shell syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns Not Present
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match ordinary prose and human rating scale text, not cryptographic code. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are the skill description and annotation scale descriptions, so the static pattern does not correspond to cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Key File Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The certificate or key file finding maps to a Python dictionary iteration over baseline metric keys. It does not reference certificate material, private keys, or credential files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is baseline.keys() inside a regression detector example, with no filesystem path or secret material.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance findings map to the word Accuracy in an evaluation dimension and a resource filename for an evaluation framework. Neither line performs scanning, probing, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation labels, not commands or API calls that enumerate hosts or services.

content.value_statement: LLM teams need repeatable ways to measure quality before changes reach users. This skill helps structure automated metrics, human review, judging, benchmarks, and regression checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains automated metrics for generation, classification, and retrieval tasks.
Shows how to structure human annotation forms and agreement checks.
Provides LLM-as-judge patterns for single response and pairwise review.
Covers A/B testing with statistical significance and effect size analysis.
Describes regression detection for tracking quality drops over time.
Outlines benchmark execution and result aggregation for model comparisons.
limitations: It is a guidance file, not a packaged evaluation library.
Example code depends on external Python packages that are not included.
LLM-as-judge examples may send evaluation data to an external API.
Metric choices still need domain-specific validation and representative data.

use_cases.title: Validate Prompt Changes
use_cases.description: Compare old and new prompts with metrics, human review, and regression checks before release.

use_cases.title: Benchmark Model Options
use_cases.description: Run consistent test sets across candidate models and summarize quality differences.

use_cases.title: Design Review Workflows
use_cases.description: Create annotation forms, scoring dimensions, and inter-rater agreement checks for human reviewers.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new evaluation plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose evaluation metrics for an LLM application that handles [task]. Include automated metrics, human review criteria, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an evaluation dataset plan for [application]. Include sample categories, expected outputs, edge cases, and minimum sample sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Prompts
prompt_templates.scenario: Assess whether a prompt change improves quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an A/B evaluation plan for prompt A and prompt B. Include metrics, human scoring, significance checks, and release criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Regression Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a continuous LLM regression testing workflow for [system]. Include baselines, thresholds, dashboards, failure triage, and governance.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a support chatbot before launch.
output_examples.output: A plan with accuracy, relevance, safety, and helpfulness metrics; representative support scenarios; human review rubrics; and launch thresholds.

output_examples.input: Compare two retrieval prompts for a RAG assistant.
output_examples.output: A comparison workflow using retrieval precision, answer groundedness, pairwise judging, error categories, and regression checks.

output_examples.input: Set up monitoring for model updates.
output_examples.output: A regression strategy with baseline scores, alert thresholds, benchmark runs, reviewer sampling, and release decision rules.

best_practices: Use several metrics because one score rarely captures full LLM quality.
Test on representative examples that include edge cases and common failures.
Combine automated results with human review for high-impact workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize only for a single automated metric.
Do not draw release conclusions from a small or biased test set.
Do not send sensitive evaluation data to external judge models without approval.

faq.question: Does this skill run evaluations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and example structures for building an evaluation workflow.

faq.question: Can it compare two prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains A/B testing, pairwise judging, statistical checks, and release criteria.

faq.question: Does it cover human evaluation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes rating dimensions, annotation form structure, and inter-rater agreement.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is tool-agnostic and can support workflows in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What data should I avoid using?
faq.answer: Avoid confidential, regulated, or user-identifying data unless your evaluation environment is approved for it.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The examples may call external model APIs, so teams should control what evaluation data leaves their environment.
